title: Register of the crops of crown lands
summary: Register of the various crops grown on crown lands at Kerkeosiris in 114/113 B.C., with statements of the amounts of rent due the crown.

title: Register of unusable crown land
summary: Register of unusable crown land in Kerkeosiris in 114/113 B.C., detailing the reason why the land was unusable and the amounts of rent lost to the crown.
translation_text: The 4th year from Menches village scribe of Kerkeosiris. Report of unprofitable land divided in (land that went out of production) since the 40th year and that until the 39th year, stating also the (arouras) that have been reported in the presence of the dioiketes after the crop report of the 4th year, and likewise the (land) that can be brought into rental from the 5th year according to its value. (Unproductive land) since the 40th year 34 1/6 1/16 arouras at a (lost) rent of 1775 1/2 1/3 1/12, and until the 39th year 596 1/4 1/8 1/16 at a (lost) rent of 3063 1/2 1/16 artabas, total of unproductive land 936 1/2 arouras 4838 1/2 1/4 artabas. After deducting the arouras that were reported to the dioiketes after the crop report of the 4th year according to its value: from the land flooded in the 52nd year, 6 1/2 arouras, and from the land unsown in the 41st year, 8 1/2 arouras, total 15 arouras, the rent of which was 75 artabas, instead of which (it is now rented out) according to its value for 5 years at 1/2 (artaba per aroura) and for the remaining time at 1, there remain of derelict land 921 1/2 arouras at a (lost) rent of 4763 1/2 1/4, of which from the 40th year 325 1/8 1/16 at a (lost) rent of 1700 1/2 1/3 1/12 artabas and until the 39th year 596 1/4 1/8 1/16 arouras at a rent of 363 1/2 1/12 artabas. Of which there can be put into rental from the derelict land set for the 5th year according to its value for pasture and pasture land from the derelict land since the 40th year, 30 arouras, the rent of which was 141 1/2 1/16 artabas, instead of which (it will now be rented out) for five years at 1/4 (artaba per aroura) and for 5 more years at 1/2 and for the remainder of the time at 1. Of these 5 1/2 1/8 1/16 come from the land flooded in the 51st year, at a (lost) rent of 28 1/2 1/4 artabas, from the land that became dry in the 40th year, 11 1/16 the rent of which was 45 artabas, from the land unsown that dried up 13 1/4 arouras at a (lost) rent of 67 1/2 1/3 artabas, total 30 arouras the rent of which was 141 1/2 1/12 artabas. The 4th year from Menches village scribe of Kerkeosiris. Report of unprofitable land divided in (land that went out of production) since the 40th year and that until the 39th year, stating also the (arouras) that were added in the presence of the dioiketes after the crop report of the 4th year, and likewise the (land) that can be brought into rental and is without profit since the 40th year, from the 5th year according to its value. Flooded by the influx of water lying nearby from the large canal in the territory of Theogonis and Tali, in the 51st year 36 1/4 arouras at a (lost) rent of 197 1/2 1/4 artabas; in the 44th year 10 arouras, 49 2/3 artabas, total 46 1/4 arouras 247 1/3 1/12 artabas. Salted land that was reported by Dionysios son of Ptolemaios, the overseer from the derelict land by Ptolemaios son of Philinos in the 50th year 61 1/2 arouras, 291 1/2 artabas, in the 44th year 9 1/4 arouras, 45 artabas; in the 42nd year 18 1/2 1/4 arouras, 76 1/2 1/3 1/12 artabas; in the 40th year 159 1/4 1/8 arouras 899 1/2 artabas, total of salted land 248 1/4 1/8 arouras, 1311 1/2 1/3 1/12 artabas. Total for derelict land since the 40th year 295 1/8 1/16 arouras at a (lost) rent of 1559 1/3 artabas. And of derelict land until the 39th year: salted land which was also worn away by the bringing upon it of water, 329 1/4 arouras 1610 1/3 artabas; flooded 192 1/2 1/4 arouras 1029 1/3 1/12 artabas, dry land because it lies next to the remainder of the land with no produce 22 1/4 1/8 1/16 94 1/2 1/4 artabas. Dry in the athetized temple land 5 arouras, 24 1/2 1/3 artabas, total 549 1/4 1/8 1/16 2759 1/3 artabas. And listed in the category of derelict land are also of land measured out to certain katoikoi instead of from the dry land as ordered, 47 arouras 234 1/12 artabas, and from the difference still awaiting a decision 70 1/6 artabas, total 47 arouras 304 1/4 artabas. Total of derelict land until the 39th year 596 1/4 1/8 1/16 arouras, 363 1/2 1/12 art. Total of derelict land without produce 891 1/2 arouras at a (lost) rent of 4622 1/6 artabas.

title: Register of payers of salt tax
summary: List of payers of salt tax (halike) in the village of Berenikis Thesmophorou.

title: Petition from Diodotos, Hesiodos and Argaios.
summary: Three brothers petition the strategos about the encroachment of a plot of land that belongs to them.
translation_text: To Ptolemaios, chief of the body-guard and strategos, from Diodotos and Hesiodos and Argaios, Thracians, inhabiting Berenikis Thesmophorou. We presented to you a petition in the 6th year against N.N. because of his design perforce to build over the unoccupied plot at the said village bequeathed by our father for us to inhabit; this the people of Diodotos observed and sent a notification to Aristodemos, who was posted on the spot, so that he might order the accused to present himself before you. He came by night with ... and brought builders to the plot and built a wall against our tower ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Petition from Herieus to the strategos : copy
summary: Herieus petitions the strategos, requesting to have the plot he inherited from his father, but that was taken away by other people, returned to him. The text is a copy made on a roll containing similar copies of petitions.
translation_text: To Ptolemaios, a chief of the body-guard and strategos, from Herieus son of Harphaesis, a cultivator of Crown land, living a Psinteo. My father having died when I was still young, Thareus, a woman of Thebes, doing violence to me, and taking as her assistants Diotimos the agent of Demetrios the superintendent of the gift-estate, and Heliodoros the ..., in defiance of all right built a tower ten cubits high on the unoccupied plot which came to me from my father. But the ordinance declares "If any person build upon the land of another, let him be deprived of the building." And whereas Thareus has since died and a certain Petesouchos and his sister Kamous lay claim to her property, I request you to summon them and to inquire into this matter, and, if it be as I say, to compel them to leave the plot. If this is done, I shall receive relief by your means. To Apollonios. Bring them up. The 10th year, Pharmouthi 29.

title: Petition
summary: Petition to the strategos from the overseer of the temple of Ammon in Moeris, relating the troubles that the temple has experienced.
translation_text: To N.N., a chief of the body-guard and strategos, from N.N., overseer of the temple of Ammon at Moeris of the 45-arouras holders. The shrine in the said temple having been (destroyed) by the men of Antiochos in ... of the 2nd year, and the ground having later been regained (?), the temple was restored to its ancient state. Afterwards when the Egyptian rebels had attacked it and not only thrown down parts of the temple but split the stone-work of the shrine and carried off the door-fixtures and other doors to the number of more than 110 and also torn down some of the boarding, after some time ... I came forward and stopped up all the gates and breaches in order that the remaining colonnades might be held together. But now ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Petition from Heliodoros to the Epimeletes.
summary: Heliodoros petitions the epimeletes, complaining that a malicious accusation was made against him.
translation_text: To Chairemon, one of the diadochoi and epimeletes, from Heliodoros son of Apollonios, of Krokodilon polis. I am the cultivator of 55 1/2 1/4 arouras of Crown land at Boubastos, of which the rent falling due from me at the rate of 4 1/2 1/12 is 254 1/2 1/3 1/12 artabas, and I have busied myself night and day with the irrigation of these owing to the breach of the dyke at Boubastos, in order not to miss the right season. But a certain Zenon, who temporarily is living, like myself, at Krokodilon polis, mischievously presented a petition against me to the local judges of Crown and private cases ... Farewell. (2nd hand) To Heriemounis. Inquire into the matter to see if it is true. To Thoonis: Bring them before Sokmenis. ... (Addressed on the verso) To Thoonis, epistates.

title: Petition
summary: Conclusion of a petition of a person seeking to recover the expenses of the maintenance of some children, and asking that his case shall be sent for trial by the chrematistai.
translation_text: ... (I beg you to send my petition to the local) chrematistai, whose clerk is Chairemon, in order that they, having selected it and summoned ..., may give judgement that he pay me the minimum amount of the expense of maintenance and other necessaries for the children from the aforesaid date, thirty-eight talents of copper, tal. 38; and I will supply the details of which this amount is made up at the trial. With regard to my claims against him I will give proper account; and if this is done, I shall obtain your succour.

title: Account of expenditure.
summary: Account listing payments in money for various purposes.

title: Account
summary: Account listing amounts in money.

title: Petition
summary: Conclusion of a petition about an alleged theft of a garment.
translation_text: ... (Having entered) the bath with a number of other persons he went off with my outer garment. I therefore ask you, if you see fit, to order a letter to be sent to the proper official to summon him before you, in order that he may be punished for this at the tribunal and by this punishment you may deter others from like courses. If this is done, I shall have found energetic succour at your hands. Farewell.

title: Petition to the strategos.
summary: The crown tenants of Oxyrhyncha petition the strategos, complaining that they could not perform additional cultivation imposed on them by a certain Apollonios due to a deficient water supply.

title: Petition from Harbichis and others to the strategos.
summary: Harbichis and the crown tenants from Oxyrhyncha petition the strategos about some unknown business possibly relating to extortion by certain officials.
translation_text: To Ptolemaios son of Purrhos, one of the first friends and strategos, from Harbichis son of Horos, komarches of Oxyrhyncha, and Harsiesis son of Pasis and Petos son of N.N. and Petos son of Harbeschinis and Metatris son of N.N. and N.N. son of Nechthenibis and N.N. son of Paimoi[-] and N.N. son of Pais and Semtheus son of Pasos and Thoonis son of N.N. and Onnophris son of Petesouchos and Tothoes son of N.N. and Sarapion son of Psenethotes and Phibion son of N.N., elders of the farmers, and from the rest of the inhabitants of the said village. In consequence of ... by certain people and other similar events you were sent to the nome in the month of Hathur of the .. year by the king and queen to put a stop to such occurrences. In accordance with the instructions which they gave you, having visited all the districts you afforded succour to the oppressed, and appointed epistatai who were worthy of that office, and likewise at the time of the gathering of the crops taking care that no one ... should have extortion practised upon him you sent orders to the epistatai to allow no one to make extortions from the Crown cultivators or others ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Documentary text
summary: Contents unclear; possibly testament or donatio mortis causa, or marriage or divorce?

title: Petition
summary: Petition from crown tenants, most likely those of Oxyrhyncha, complaining about official repression which included not only the seizure of a quantity of grain, but resort to torture.

title: Petition from Petosiris to the acting strategos.
summary: Petosiris and fellow superintendents of the precinct of Arsinoe petition the acting strategos, asking them to inforce a royal order for their protection, which had been neglected by others to that moment.
translation_text: To Arkas, one of the first friends and chief chamberlains, who is performing the duties of strategos, from Petosiris son of Spagis and his fellow superintendents of the precinct of Arsinoe at Oxyrhyncha in the division of Polemon. Some of the tax payers from the village, and others, forcing their way into the aforesaid precinct not only to impose insolence and injury ... We therefore having sailed down to Alexandria presented a petition to the king and queen in which we set forth a statement similar to the above; this was sent on to [-]llides, who was strategos, having the command attached ... that neither the precinct nor the superintendents should be injured but should be left undisturbed in accordance with their request. The [superintendents] also ... wrote accordingly to the epistates of the village, and likewise presented many memoranda to you on the same subject, in response to which you wrote to Ptolemaios, the epistates of the village, to allow no one to do anything improper and to produce the persons named. Up to the present, however, no notice has been taken. We beg that henceforth the command of the king and queen should be applied to the precinct and that a copy of this should be engraved upon the outer gate of the precinct below the existing tablet in order that no one may force their way in by it, and request you, if you think fit, to allow us to carry out the command ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Account of expenditure
summary: Account listing payments in money for various purposes, among which wine.

title: Account
summary: Names, numbers, money

title: Petition to the strategos.
summary: The Crown tenants of Oxyrhyncha petition the strategos, complaining about official repression by an oikonomos.

title: Unidentified document.
summary: Possibly account or letter.
genre: Accounts, Ptolemaic Period; Business letters, Ptolemaic Period

title: Petition to the Overseer of the revenues.
summary: Petesouchos and the other Crown tenants petition the overseer of the revenues, complaining that an order given by him was being disregarded by another official.
translation_text: To Asklepiades, one of those equal in rank to the king's cousins and overseer of the revenues, from Petesouchos son of Petos, komarches, and the Crown tenants associated with him, from Oxyrhyncha in the division of Polemon. We presented to you another petition about writing to Asklepiades the local sitologos-in-chief to accept for our dues 200 artabas of Greek reeds. This having been done you gave orders to send these and the other loans for the seed of the crown land to the land. But now Asklepiades contrary to your instructions ... Farewell.

title: Register of correspondence.
summary: Copies of correspondence, mostly petitions addressed to Horos, village scribe of Berenikis Thesmophorou.
translation_text: (Col. I, lines 19-30) I have appended for your information a copy of the notice delivered to me by Adymos son of Adymos, eighty-arouras-holder. Goodbye. The 22nd year, Tybi .. To Horos, komogrammateus of Berenikis Thesmophorou, from Adymos, Macedonian, holder of eighty arouras. On the night preceding the 2nd of Tybi certain persons having made an incursion into the quarters belonging to me opened the room in the courtyard and stole a ... mill which I value at 500 drachmas and from the courtyard three young sheep worth 600 drachmas. I therefore send notice to you, in order that you may make inquiry into the same. Goodbye. The 22nd year, Tybi .. (col. VI, lines 12-26) Phamenoth 23. To Harmais, topogrammateus. I have subjoined copies of the notices delivered to me on the 2[.]th of the present month from Timon, leader of the men from Asia, and Phanesis, donkey-driver, in order that you may be informed and give proper heed. To Horos, Komogrammateus, from Timon, leader of the men from Asia. On the night preceding the 2[.]th of Phamenoth in the 22nd year certain persons got over into the house of Phanesis the donkey-driver and drove off a black donkey which I had hired out to Phanesis and the son of Phanesis and valued to Phanesis and his son at 1 talent. I beg you to set my notice on the list. (Col. VIII, lines 4-9) Another from Herakleides. On the night preceding the ...th of Epeiph of the 22nd year certain persons got over on to the house of Ptolemaios in which I live and (possessed themselves?) of the best of two beasts of burden belonging to me, which I value at .. talents of bronze. I have delivered a notice to the guards. (Col. VIII, lines 17-28) Another from Pnepheros son of Harkoiphis, Crown tenant from Berenikis Thesmophorou. I am wronged by Ptolemaios son of Sosipatros, hundred-arouras-holder. On the 24th of Epeiph of the 22nd year, I sent my son Pnepheros to ... with 3 pairs of oxen and when he wished to cross the bridge in the road the aforesaid Ptolemaios violently seizing the boy's stick drove them into the canal and one of the three cows, worth 2000 drachmas, happened to be killed by a crocodile. I beg you, if you think fit, to order a letter to be written to the proper official to compel restitution to be made so that I may be able to cultivate my land for the 23rd year. The 22nd year, Epeiph 2[.]th. (Col. XI, line 3 - Col. XII line 4) ... On the 2nd of the aforesaid month at a late hour Dorion the desert guard arriving at his house in the street opposite the shrine of Boubastis came upon Hesiodos son of Didymos, hundred-arouras-holder, at the door, and unpleasantness having arisen, Dorion ... the nose of the aforesaid Hesiodos [slitting?] the nostril and cut (?) his lip ...; and Hesiodos cut the right ear of Dorion clean off ... We have appended for your information the notice delivered to us by Hesiodos. The 22nd year, Mesore 2nd. To Horos, komogrammateus of Berenikis Thesmophorou from Hesiodos, Thracian, 100-arouras-holder of the 5th hipparchy. On the 2nd of Mesore of the 22nd year, as I was returning home at a late hour and had arrived at the Boubastis-shrine of Patsontis, Dorion son of Dionysios, one of the desert guards, attacked me along with certain others and gave me many blows with the sword he had, so that I received many wounds and my nostril [was slit?]. Wherefore I have sent notice both to you and to the guards. ... An outcry being raised ... he took to flight ... my life is in danger. The 22nd year, Mesore 4. (Col. XII, lines 17-21) Likewise from Alexandros. The produce in wine of Elpines son of Elpines for the 22nd year, 12 6-chous jars, 1 8-chous, 8 5-chous, total 20 six-chous jars, he has sold to meet his debts to Petosiris son of Sokonopis, a ... from Berenikis, at 275 drachmas each, which makes 5,500. I have therefore written for your information.

title: Notification of loss.
summary: Petosiris notifies the phulakites about loss.

title: Register of land
summary: Part of an account concerning crown land.
translation_text: ... land leased for copper money by Chairemon, one of the diadochi and epimeletes, 390 1/16 1/32 arouras, of which 30 arouras belong to the land leased for grain, yielding, at 1 artaba, 30 artabas, the remainder being 360 1/16 1/32 ar., of which there is a lease till the 28th year to Dionysios at a total for the nome of 1 talent 4500 drachmas of copper.

title: Notification to archiphylakites.
summary: Athenodora informs the archiphylakites that her house had been entered in her absence.
translation_text: The .. year, Thoth 2. Notification to Dioskourides, chief of the guard of Krokodilon polis and the associated districts, from Athenodora daughter of Hestiodoros. On the 2nd of the above month, after I had shut the door of the men's apartments and the staircase and the yard-doors of my house, which is in the said city near the so-called courthouse of the laokritai, and had gone out to the bath ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Petition to the archiphylakites
summary: Poregebthis petitions the archiphulakites of Berenikis Thesmophorou, complaining that he had been molested and robbed.

title: Account
summary: Account listing amounts in money.

title: Petition
summary: Petition to an unknown official concerning a water conduit that had been filled up during the absence of the owner.
translation_text: ... after my said father had been summoned on the king's business, on the 24th of Epeiph in the 27th year Herakleon with others, of whose names I am ignorant, came to the water channel, which my father had constructed at great expense in the 30-arouras holding of Menelaos, and improperly blocked it up. Accordingly I present to you this notice and beg you ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Petition to the komogrammateus.
summary: Sabattaios petitions the komogrammateus of Samareia, complaining that his wife had been attacked and injured by another woman.
translation_text: To N.N. komogrammateus of Samareia (?), from Sabattaios son of N.N., a Jew and one of the workmen (?) and wage-earners (?) of the said village ... in consequence of the blows and the fall she is suffering severely and having had to take to her bed her unborn child is in danger of dying and being miscarried. I present to you this petition in order that, when you have visited the spot and observed her (?) condition, Joanna may be secured until the result is apparent and that it may not happen that Joanna in case of any untoward event goes scot-free. The 28th year, Pauni 21. (2nd hand) Registered (?) Pauni 25.

title: Official letter
summary: Official letter in which one official informs another about the disappearance of a number of hides.

title: Petition to the hipparches.
summary: Paalas petitions the hipparches, complaining that certain people had damaged his ship and assaulted himself.
translation_text: The 36th year, Hathur 11. To Demetrios, one of the diadochoi, hipparches over men and epistates, from Paalas son of Harmais, ship's guard of the barge of Apollonios, one of the first friends and strategos and overseer of the revenues. On the (30th?) of Phaophi of the 36th year, the said barge being at anchor, certain persons approaching in their own boat came to anchor outside it (?) and broke away some of our gear, and when I rebuked them so that they might keep clear, they leapt on board with unseemly shouts and gave me many blows, with the result that in the tussle I lost a cloak worth 3000 drachmas of bronze as well as the broken gear, which was worth likewise 3000 drachmas of bronze. I beg you therefore to give orders ... Farewell.

title: Petition to the Hipparches
summary: The Crown tenants of Oxyrhyncha petition the hipparches, mentioning a great reduction in their number by various acts of injustice.
translation_text: To Aminias, one of the diadochoi, hipparches and epistates of Oxyrhyncha, from the Crown cultivators of the said village. Whereas we were formerly 140 men but because of the injustices done to us are 40 in all, although ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Petition to the epistates.
summary: Petition to the epistates of Tebtunis, reporting the burglary of a house.
translation_text: To Poseidonios, epistates of Tebtunis, from Pa[--] son of Pasis, cultivator from the said village. On the night preceding the 5th of Phamenoth of the 5th year certain persons forced their way into my house and having undermined the doorpost entered the vestibule. I woke up and shouted for help ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Unidentified document.
summary: Unknown, possibly an account.

title: Receipts for fodder : copies or drafts
summary: Two receipts for fodder, the first for fodder received from Petosiris, the second from Semtheus.
translation_text: 1st receipt: Lysis, keeper of horses and antigrapheus, to Petosiris [son of ?], greeting. I have received from you for the royal horses kept by me at Krokodilon polis in the Arsinoite nome, in the month of Tybi of the 29th year, 2000 two-minai bundles of hay. 2nd Receipt: Lysis, keeper of horses and antigrapheus to Semtheus son of Phthaus, greeting. I have received from you for the royal horses kept by me at Krokodilon polis in the Arsinoite nome, in the month of Tybi of the 29th year, one thousand five hundred two-minai bundles of hay, total 1500.

title: Petition to the komarches.
summary: Ptolemaios petitions the komarches concerning some grievance against another farmer to whom he had sub-let part of his holding.
translation_text: To Petesouchos, komarches of Oxyrhyncha, from Ptolemaios son of N.N., Crown cultivator from the said village. I leased to Petesouchos son of Teos, an inhabitant of the said village, out of the Crown land which I cultivate at the village 3 1/4 1/8 arouras for the 4th year at a rent per aroura of 5 talents of bronze, on condition that he should sow it with garlic, by an Egyptian contract of lease, having agreed with him that he should pay before the 30th (?) of Phamenoth of the 4th year or else should measure to the royal grainary towards the rent of the land on my behalf, for the residue of the bronze, wheat at the rate of 700 bronze drachmas per artaba. The accused Petesouchos son of Teos ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.) (Addressed on the verso) To Petesouchos, komarches of Oxyrhyncha.

title: Declaration of property.
summary: Eutychos declares his horse to the basilikos grammateus.
translation_text: To N.N. basilikos grammateus, from Eutychos son of Eutychos, Acarnanian of the 100-arouras-holders of Demetrios and his sons in the 4th hipparchy. I return the chestnut female horse that belongs to me. Let this return be placed on record for me, in order that I may not subsequently be calumniated. Farewell. The 31st year, Mesore 21. (2nd hand) ... The 31st year, Mesore 23.

title: Official letter : draft or copy
summary: The papyrus is too damaged to give any idea about the contents.

title: Application for lease of Crown land.
summary: Fragment of an offer to lease crown land, the rent of which was to be increased.

title: Official letter : draft or copy.
summary: Official letter in which an official reports about the state of affairs regarding the piece of land which forms the subject of the recto.

title: Memorandum
summary: Mysthes submits this memorandum in order to have a plot of land transferred to the new owner.
translation_text: ... [The boundaries are on the south] the holding of N.N. which has been confiscated, on the north the guard's holding of Theodoros, on the west the Crown land cultivated by N.N. son of N.N., on the east the land of Ph[--] son of N.N., and I have received from him the wages for the land and the expenses. I therefore present to you the memorandum in order that you may transfer the 3 arouras to the name of Ptolemaios in the sowing-list of the said year and in the survey according to crops through the usual officials, as is right. The 30th year, Choiak 19. (2nd hand) I, Mysthes, will hand over the 3 arouras as aforesaid.

title: Declaration on oath
summary: The declaration is concerned with the consequences of a divorce.
genre: Declarations, Ptolemaic Period; Oaths, Ptolemaic Period
translation_text: ... thirty-six drachmas of silver, total 36 dr. silver, and four quarter of gold, total 4 qrs. gold, on condition that she (?) annuls with me our contract of cohabitation. Good-bye. The 25th year, Tubi 21. I swear by king Ptolemy and queen Cleopatra his sister and their ancestors that I will act accordingly.

title: Declaration on oath.
summary: Declaration by a shipper, the precise contents of which is unclear owing to the mutilation of the papyrus.

title: Declaration on oath.
summary: Declaration to a contractor for the tax on sales, concerning a manumission of a slave.
genre: Declarations, Ptolemaic Period; Oaths, Ptolemaic Period

title: Offer for post with tax-farmers.
summary: Apollonios offers to pay to the farmers of the tax on sales a sum of money, in return for which he wishes to learn the business of the tax on sales.
translation_text: To N.N., Aniketos, Ptolemaios and Isidoros, contractors for the tax on sales for the 14th year, from Apollonios son of Hermogenes. I undertake to pay for the initiation of the tax on sales, on condition that, taking the proper receipts as regards the banker and agoranomos, I [rightly?] conduct with them the business of the individuals so that the contract incurs no loss, 500 drachmas a month, which makes 1 talent for the year. ... will act with me ... [The 14th year, Thoth ?] 15.

title: Account of payments in kind
summary: Account listing persons and payments made by them.

title: Account
summary: Unknown. The writing is too damaged to render any sense.

title: Register of sale of forfeited property.
summary: Register in which copies of documents relating to the official disposal to a woman of certain property to which she had right of execution, followed by a short register of tax payments due and paid.
translation_text: [Copy of part of an assignment: 8th year, (month, day) at Krokodilon polis] in the Arsinoite nome, by the agency of the auctioneer Andronikos, Boiskos, the subordinate of Alexander, collector, assigned a vineyard with a press in it. Another part of the same assignment: the property designated by Therous daughter of Nektathumis, Arsinoite, along with her guardian Herakleides son of Apollonios, Persian under employ. Another part of the same assignment: Therous having made a designation along with the same Herakleides. Copy of part of a sale: 8th year, Gorpiaios 2, Phaophi 1, at Krokodilon polis in the Arsinoite nome, Nikolaos being agoranomos. Therous daughter of Nektathumis, Arsinoite, about 50 years of age, light-skinned, round-faced, short, straight-nosed, with a scar on the right eyebrow, with her guardian Herakleides son of Apollonios, Persian under employ, about 45 years of age, light-skinned, short, bow-legged, thin-bearded, bought. Another part of the same sale: by an assignment a house and gatehouse and bath-house. Copy of part of another sale: 8th year, Gorpiaios 2, Phaophi 1, at Krokodilon polis in the Arsinoite nome, Nikolaos being agoranomos. Therous daughter of Nektathumis, Arsinoite, about 50 years of age, light-skinned, round-faced, short, straight-nosed, with a scar on the right eyebrow, with her guardian Herakleides son of Apollonios, Persian under employ, about 45 years of age, light-skinned, short, bow-legged, thin-bearded, bought by an assignment from Boiskos the subordinate of Alexandros, collector, a vineyard. Copy of part of record of payment: 8th year, Mesore 25. Sokrates acknowledges that Therous daughter of Nektathumis, Arsinoite, with her guardian Herakleides son of Apollonios, Persian under employ, has paid to the Crown at the bank of Python at Krokodilon polis the tax on a house and gatehouse and bath-house. Copy of part of the certificate of sequestration: [8?]th year, Daisios 8. Therous daugther of Nektathymis, Arsinoite, with her guardian Herakleides son of Apollonios, Persian under employ, designated to Boiskos, subordinate of Alexandros, collector, a vineyard, in which is a press, by Syron kome, the property of Petesouchos son of Psenithes, Syro-Egyptian, for the execution upon him devolving upon her in accordance with a sentence of the chrematistai ... (Col. II) Return of salt-tax for the .. year through Taterous: Taterous daughter of Phanesis, general dealer; Thasis daughter of Semtheus, her daughter; total, 2 females. Likewise Herakleides son of Apollonios, Apollonios his son, Dionysodoros his son, Ammonios his son; total, 4. Taterous daughter of Phanesis, general dealer; Thasis daughter of Semtheus, her daughter; total, 2 females. Likewise Herakleides son of Apollonios, Apollonios his son, Dionysodoros his son, Ammonios his son. Copy of a bank-receipt. 20th year, Tubi 29. Paid to the collecting-office in the city to Damasias agent of Theoxenos, for the Crown, by Thasis daugther of Semtheus the renewal-tax on a house and appurtenances at Krokodilon polis which she stated that she pledged to ... for 480 drachmas of bronze, and an additional sum (?) up to Choiak of the 19th year of 108 drachmas, ... up to Choiak of the 20th year 108 dr., total 696, namely 13 dr. 5 1/2 obols of bronze equated to silver.

title: Declaration of unwatered land
summary: Sambas reports that his cleruchic holding has gone unirrigated.
translation_text: To Philotades alias Demetrios, basilikos grammateus of the Division of Polemon of the Arsinoite nome and to Chrysas, komogrammateus of Tebtunis, from Sambas, son of Sambas, of the aforesaid village of Tebtunis. I register in accordance with the commands of his excellency the prefect Subatianus Aquila the 6 arouras of cleruchic land which belong to me at the aforesaid village in the place called after Tasikrates, rated at 1 artaba of wheat each, as having been unirrigated for the present year. Wherefore I make this return. (2nd hand) Registered with Paesis in the 16th year, Phamenoth 25. (3rd hand) I, Chrysas, komogrammateus, have received a duplicate of this for examination.

title: Loan of money.
summary: Ioudas has lent a substantial sum of money to Agathocles, it being the debt still remaining from an earlier loan from Ioudas to Agathocles.
translation_text: In the reign of Ptolemaios son of Ptolemaios and Kleopatra the gods Epiphanai in the seventh year, while Philostratos son of Asklepiodotos was priest of Alexandros and the gods Soteres and the gods Adelphoi and the gods Euergetai and the gods Philopatores and the gods Epiphanai, and Aspasia daughter of Chrysermos was athlophoros of Berenike Euergetis, and Isidora daugther of Apollonios kanephoros of Arsinoe Philadelphos, and Eirene daughter of Ptolemaios priestess of Arsinoe Philopator, on the thirteenth of the month Gorpiaios, (is) the thirteenth of Phamenoth, in Trikomia in the division of Themistos of the Arsinoite nome. Ioudas son of Iosephos, Jew of the Epigone, has lent to Agathokles son of Ptolemaios, Jew, taktomisthos of the infantry of Molossos stationed in the Herakleopolite nome, two talents five hundred drachmas of bronze money for twelve months from the date above written with interest at two drachmas per mina per month. This loan is the amount which Agathokles still owed to Ioudas out of five talents which he had received from Ioudas as an advance towards a money-changing (?) business in partnership according to a written agreement, of which Ananias son of Ionathan, Jew of the Epigone, is the guardian. Agathokles shall repay to Ioudas the aforesaid loan and the interest in the month of Mecheir of the eighth year; but if he do repay it as stated, he shall pay it increased by a half. This agreement is valid. Witnesses are Deinias son of Aineas, Thraseas son of Sosibios, Thebon son of Phanokles, Samaelos son of Ioannes, all four Jews of the Epigone, Theodoros son of Theodoros who is also called Samaelos, Nikanor son of Iason, the two Jews of the men through Dositheos, of the first hipparchy, eighty-arouras men. (2nd hand) I, Agathokles, have received the two talents and the five hundred drachmas, the amount of the loan above written, and have deposited the agreement, which is valid, with Deinias as its keeper.

title: Cession of quarters.
summary: Theodotos acknowledges to have ceded to four Crown tenants the quarters belonging to his father. The six witnesses are all Jews.
translation_text: Scriptura interior: In the reign of Ptolemaios son of Ptolemaios and Arsinoe, gods Philopatores, in the 4th year, while Adaios son of Gorgias was priest of Alexandros and the gods Soteres and the gods Adelphoi and the gods Euergetai and the gods Philopatores, and Philte daughter of Antikles was athlophoros of Berenike Euergetis, and Arsinoe daughter of Solon kanephoros of Arsinoe Philadelphos, in the month Audnaios, which is Epeiph in the Egyptian calendar, on the fifteenth, in Samareia in the Arsinoite nome. Theodotos son of Kassandros, a Paeonian of Phyleus' troop, taktomisthos, acknowledges ro Haryotes son of Harseus and to Orsenouphis son of N.N. and to Katytis son of Pet[--] abd to another Katytis son of P[--], all four Arsinoites and farmers of Crown land, that he has ceded the quarters belonging to his father in Kerkesephis ... and that he will not proceed against them himself or by any agent of his nor will suborn any other person to do so on any pretext. And if he himself or any agent of his proceeds against them ... Theodotos shall forfeit to Haryotes and Orsenouphis and Katytis and the other Katytis a fine of 2000 drachmas in bronze coin, and his claim shall be invalid. This contract shall be valid. Witnesses are Theodotos son of Iason, Diophantos son of Theodotos, Hieroous son of Timotheos, Milon son of Zosimos, Demokrates son of N.N., Sabbathaios son of N.N., all six Jews. Keeper of the contract is Hieroous son of Timotheos. Scriptura exterior: In the reign of Ptolemaios son of Ptolemaios and Arsinoe, gods Philopatores, in the 4th year, while Adaios son of Gorgias was priest of Alexandros and the gods Soteres and the gods Adelphoi and the gods Euergetai and the gods Philopatores, and Philte daughter of Antikles was athlophoros of Berenike Euergetis, and Arsinoe daughter of Solon kanephoros of Arsinoe Philadelphos, in the month Audnaios, which is Epeiph in the Egyptian calendar, on the fifteenth, in Samareia in the Arsinoite nome. Theodotos son of Kassandros, a Paeonian of Phyleus' troop, taktomisthos, acknowledges ro Haryotes son of Harseus and to Orsenouphis son of N.N. and to Katytis son of Pet[--] abd to another Katytis son of P[--], all four Arsinoites and farmers of Crown land, that he has ceded the quarters belonging to his father in Kerkesephis ... and that he will not proceed against them himself or by any agent of his nor will suborn any other person to do so on any pretext. And if he himself or any agent of his proceeds against them ... Theodotos shall forfeit to Haryotes and Orsenouphis and Katytis and the other Katytis a fine of 2000 drachmas in bronze coin, and his claim shall be invalid. This contract shall be valid. Witnesses are Theodotos son of Iason, Diophantos son of Theodotos, Hieroous son of Timotheos, Milon son of Zosimos, Demokrates son of N.N., Sabbathaios son of N.N., all six Jews. Keeper of the contract is Hieroous son of Timotheos.

title: Withdrawal of claims
summary: Histieia acknowledges she will make no more accusations against Taouthes; the document is a cheirographon.
translation_text: Histieia daughter of Histieios, Macedonian, to Taouthes daughter of Marres, greeting. In regard to the accusation which I have made against you before the tribunal of Aristoboulos, I am reconciled to you and make no accusation in regard to the matters whereon I have accused you and your assessors decided against you. And if I proceed against you for the outrage, or any agent of mine, I will forfeit to you 3000 drachmas. Goodbye. The 13th year, Pachon 7. Menekles wrote this on behalf of Histieia, since she affirmed that she was illiterate.

title: Unidentified.
summary: Beginning of a contract preserving only the prescript of the contract.

title: Copies of documents.
summary: Copies of documents relating to various property, likely belonging to the same family.
translation_text: I, Antiochos, ... taktomisthos, am acquainted with Demainetos the heir aforesaid of the property of his mother Myrtale daughter of Didymos, a Persian woman; to him belongs the inheritance and to none other, as aforesaid. I, Demeas son of Protarchos, of the Apian deme, of the troop of Aristoteles, taktomisthos, am acquainted with Demainetos the heir aforesaid of the property of his mother Myrtale daughter of Didymos, a Persian woman; to him belongs the inheritance and to none other, as aforesaid. Demainetos is aged about 35 years, fair, curly-haired, having a long face and rather bright eyes, ... with a scar along his left ear; Antiochus is aged about 40 year, short, fair, straight-haired, having a long face and a mole by his right eyebrow; and Demeas is aged about 45 years, of middle height, fair, straight-haired, bald on the forehead, long-faced, short-sighted, with a scar on the left eyebrow. ... N.N. to Demainetos and Hyllos and Ptolemaios, greeting. Since the house belonging to us at Herakleopolis above Memphis has been knocked down to you in pledge, sell it as you please and I who am the next of kin will not proceed against it nor you nor the purchasers nor shall another do so on my behalf on any pretext whatever. If I do any of the things above written or another on my behalf, I or the person who proceeds on my behalf will be liable to you and will further forfeit as a sacred offering 1000 drachmas of silver. Let this be valid wherever produced either by you or those in valid accord with you. Good-bye. The thirteenth year, Mecheir 24. Copy of a six-witness contract. In the reign of Ptolemaios son of Ptolemaios ... and Arsinoe, the gods Philopatores, while N.N. son of Eumelos was priest of Alexander and the gods Soteres and the gods Adelphoi and the gods Euergetai and the gods Philopatores and the gods Epiphanai, while Artemidora daughter of Diogenes was athlophoros of Berenike Euergetis, while Apollonia daughter of Athenodoros was kanephoros of Arsinoe Philadelphos, while Eirene daughter of Ptolemaios was priestess of Arsinoe Philopator, in the month Apellaios, on the twenty-ninth, in Herakleous polis. Ptolemaios, son of Amyntas, Macedonian of the troop of Hekataios, taktomisthos, acknowledges to Mesoesis son of Alexandros ... (Here the papyrys breaks off. Only few letter remaining of col. III.)

title: Account
summary: Unknown. The text is too fragmentary to render any sense.

title: Naukleros receipt.
summary: A ship's captain acknowledges to have received a cargo of wheat.

title: Register of crops of crown land
summary: Register of the various crops grown on crown lands at Kerkeosiris, with statement of the amount of rent due to the crown in 113/112 B.C.

title: Naukleros receipt.
summary: Ammonios acknowledges the receipt of a certain amount of wheat from the granary at Boubastos for shipment to Alexandria.
translation_text: The 6th year, Hathur 13. Ammonios, captain of the kerkouros of Herakleides son of Dorion, of 11,000 artabas burthen, acknowledges that he has embarked at the harbor of Ptolemais through Heraklitos the representative of the oikonomos from the store at Boubastos, out of the produce of the 5th year, destined for Alexandria for the Royal store, of the wheat purchased through Tephraios, six hundred and twenty artabas, total 620; and commuted to barley sixty five (artabas), total 65; and I make no complaint.

title: Naukleros receipt.
summary: Ammonios acknowledges the receipt of a certain amount of wheat from the granary at Boubastos for shipment to Alexandria.
translation_text: The 6th year, Hathur 13. Ammonios, captain of the kerkouros of Herakleides son of Dorion, of 11,000 artabas burthen, acknowledges that he has embarked at the harbor of Ptolemais through Apollonios the antigrapheos of the basilikogrammateos from the store at Boubastos, out of the produce of the 5th year, destined for Alexandria for the Royal store, of the wheat purchased through Theophraios, six hundred and twenty artabas, total 620; and commuted to barley sixty five (artabas), total 65; and I make no complaint.

title: Naukleros receipt.
summary: Ammonios acknowledges the receipt of a certain amount of wheat from the granary at Boubastos for shipment to Alexandria.
translation_text: The 6th year, Hathur 13. Ammonios, captain of the kerkouros of Herakleides son of Dorion, of 11,000 artabas burthen, acknowledges that he has embarked at the harbor of Ptolemais through Lysimachos the sitologos of the store at Boubastos, and the representative of N.N. basilikos grammateus, destined for Alexandria for the Royal store, out of the produce of the 5th year, six hundred and twenty artabas, total 620; and commuted to barley sixty five (artabas), total 65; and I make no complaint.

title: Account of taxes.
summary: Account listing amounts of wheat to be paid for rent and taxes.

title: Register of unproductive land.
summary: Register listing unproductive plots of land in an unknown locale, with the amount of rent lost to the Crown.

title: Register of unproductive land.
summary: Register listing the amount of unproductive land and lost revenue.
translation_text: (lines 6-15) There is also reported the unproductive category for the 41st year: land flooded owing to water discharged from the plains at Tebetnu from the outburst of the bend of the river Montilas by Hiera Nesos and the marshes by Kerkeesis and Ptolemais of the Bee-keepers which became overfull and flooded the adjacent royal land, 76 1/2 arouras 397 1/2 1/3 artabas, of which 25 1/2 were in copper, 40 in lentils, 40 in beans, and the individual items are: --of Petesouchos son of Horos, as credited, 3 arouras at 5 1/4 1/24 (artabas to the ar.), 15 1/2 1/3 art.

title: Account of payments in kind.
summary: Account listing names and amounts in wheat, possibly payments for taxes.

title: Field-by-field register of land.
summary: 1 column and remains of another one of a field-by-field register of land, giving for each plot the geographical relations to other plots, the name of the landholder and his patronymic, the fiscal category to which the plot belongs, and the crop with which it is sown.
genre: Registers of land, Ptolemaic Period; Paintings

title: Field-by-field register of land.
summary: Part of a fragments of a field-by-field register, mentioning the officials present, and giving the details of one plot of land, including the area calculations.
translation_text: Phamenoth At Ibion Argaiou, in the presence of Tothes, comogrammateus, and Peteesis, comarch, the elders of the cultivators, Apunchis, son of Apunchis, Peteesis, son of Phamounis, Belles and Sisois, phylacitai, also engaged in the garding of the crops. Beginning north and east of the fields in the area of Berenicis, Eudemos, son of Theon, 10 3/16, 3/2.5, 10 1/16 schoinia, total 27 27/32 arourai, excess 7 27/32 arourai Proceeding towards the east along the...

title: Receipt for grain-dues.
summary: Heliodoros acknowledges the receipt of dues in grain for rent of and taxes upon Crown land.
translation_text: The 30th year, Pauni [..]. Heliodoros also called N.N., sitologos of the store at Oxyrhyncha, acknowledges delivery through the assessors at Tebetnu, for account of that village, of the produce of the said year from Herakleides son of Herakleides for rent twenty three and a half artabas of wheat, total 23 1/2 art/ wheat, for the three-choinix tax 1/3 art.

title: Report concerning cleruchic land
summary: List of plots of cleruchic land for which Menches paid a tax.
translation_text: From Menches, village scribe of Kerkeosiris. The undermentioned holdings being returned as unproductive in the survey according to crops for the 5th year, I undertake to provide the artaba-tax upon them for the said year or to measure it out from my private means. Katoikoi: Polemon son of Ammonios 20 arouras, 20 artabas; Asklepiades son of Ptolemaios 24 arouras, 24 artabas; Maron son of Dionysios 25 arouras, 25 artabas; total for katoikoi 69 arouras, 69 artabas. Ephodos: Ptolemaios son of Meniskos 24 arouras, 24 artabas. In all 93 arouras, 93 artabas. 5th year, Mecheir 29.

title: Receipt for grain-dues.
summary: Tothoes and Palamounis summarize the amount of grain received at a local granary on account of rent.
translation_text: The 4th year, Pharmouthi, from Tothoes and Palamounis, sitologoi of the grain-store in external districts. Grain received on the 21st from the produce of the said year : measured into the granary at Alabanthis for that village, for seed 14 artabas of wheat, for rent 449 1/2 1/3 art. of pure wheat, for loss in cleansing 9 1/3 1/12 art., fee for sifting 4 2/3 art., making 14 1/12 art., total 463 1/2 1/3 1/12 art.

title: Receipt for guard-tax (phylakitikon)
summary: Heliodoros reports the receipt of payments for guard-tax.
translation_text: Apollodoros and Herakleides. Herakleides, sitologos of ... in the Suburb (Peri Polin), reports that he has received in Mesore, in conjunction with Dorion ..., from Theomnestos son of Timarchos, spearman, on land at Niseus for guard-tax of the 31st year ... artabas of wheat. (7 more lines.)

title: Receipt for tax on pigeon-houses (trite peristeronon)
summary: Receipt for payment of tax on pigeon-houses by Kleon, with signatures by the representatives of the basilikos grammateus and oikonomos.
translation_text: The 19th year, Epeiph 2. By ... Paid into the bank at Krokodilon polis to Ptolemaios, banker, for the king, by Kleon son of Nikomachos, for the tax of a third on pigeon-houses at Oxyrhyncha, for the 19th year, one thousand drachmas of bronze at a discount, total 1000 dr. (2nd hand) The 19th year, Epeiph 2. In the presence of N.N., agent of the basilikos grammateus, one thousand drachmas of bronze at a discount, total 1000 dr. (3rd hand) The 19th year, Epeiph 2. In the presence of Chairemon, agent of the oikonomos, 1000 drachmas of bronze at a discount, total 1000 dr.

title: Receipt for chaff.
summary: Receipt showing that the crown cultivators of Oxyrhyncha had paid their quota of chaff for the year.
translation_text: N.N. to Harsiesis, komarches of Oxyrhyncha and to the elders (of the farmers), greeting. I have received from you the moia of chaff which are assigned to us for the 30th year, namely two hundred, total 200, and I make no claim on you. Goodbye. The 31st year, Thoth 6.

title: Account of grain and other produce.
summary: Account listing names and amounts of farm produce (wheat, barley, castor seeds, etc.).

title: Account
summary: Account listing amounts in artabas.

title: Account
summary: Account detailing various receipts and expenditures regarding state grain: transport, seed-grain, and receipts.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account listing names of Crown cultivators, grouped under villages and subdivided according to days of the month, with amounts of rent and taxes to be paid by each crown tenant.

title: Granary account.
summary: Account listing payments for rent and other charges, as well as for arrears.

title: Register of unusable crown land
summary: Register of unusable crown land in Kerkeosiris in 113/112 B.C., detailing the reason why the land was unusable and the amounts of rent lost to the crown.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account listing names of Crown cultivators, grouped under villages and subdivided according to days of the month, with amounts of rent and taxes to be paid by each crown tenant.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account of grain received as payment for arrears of rent, imposts, etc.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account of grain received as payment for arrears of rent, imposts, etc.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account of grain received as payment for arrears of rent, imposts, etc.

title: Account of receipts in kind.
summary: Account listing daily deliveries of grain as payment for taxes.

title: Granary account.
summary: Accounting dealing with various matters pertaining to the business of the state granaries.

title: Receipt for transport dues.
summary: Receipt for dues, paid by Psenkebkis to the account of the nomarch, on three different occasions, for transport of sheaves (dragmategai) and sacks (sakkegai)
translation_text: The eleventh year of Imperator Caesar Nerva Trajanus Augustus Germanicus Dacicus, Pauni 22. Paid by Psenkebkis son of Pakebkis to Orses, Horion and partners (to the account) of Flavius Herakleides, nomarches, for transport of sheaves and sacks in the said 11th year eight drachmas of silver, total 8 dr.; and on the 7th of the month Epeiph eight drachmas more, total 8 dr.; and on the 8th of the same month two drachmas more, total 2 dr.

title: Account of payments in money.
summary: Account listing payments in money for various taxes by certain persons at different villages in the division of Polemon.

title: Private account.
summary: Payments for various purposes.

title: Artemidoros' letter : to Paeis
summary: Probably an arrest order.

title: Official letter from Hermias : copy
summary: Hermias, possibly the dioiketes, informs Horos about the upcoming visit of a Roman senator to the Arsinoite nome, and orders him to make the necessary preparations
translation_text: Hermias to Horos, greeting. Appended is a copy of the letter to Asklepiades. Take care that its instructions are followed. Good-bye. The fifth year, Xandikos 17, Mecheir 17. To Asklepiades. Lucius Memmius, a Roman senator, who occupies a position of great dignity and honor, is making the voyage from Alexandria to the Arsinoite nome to see the sights. Let him be received with special magnificence, and take care that at the proper spots the chambers be prepared and the landing-places to them be got ready, and that the gifts of hospitality below written be presented to him at the landing-place, and that the furniture of the chamber, the customary tit-bits for Petesouchos and the crocodiles, the necessaries for the view of the Labyrinth, and the offerings and sacrifices be provided; in general take the greatest pains in everything that the visitor may be satisfied, and display the utmost zeal... (here the papyrus breaks off)

title: Tax receipt.
summary: Receipt for payment of 7 (?) drachmas by an inhabitant of Tebtunis.

title: Tax return prepared by sitologoi
summary: Declaration of payments received at the village granary.

title: Account of grain transport.
summary: List of sacks of grain sent to or from various villages.

title: Tax account.
summary: Total taxes received in the Egyptian months of Hathyr and Choiak
translation_text: (An account for Hathur; followed by:) Choiak, 625 drachmas, of which: to me, Sotas, 28 dr. To Paktas, guard, 20 drachmas, for the price of papyrus, 9 drachmas, for bronze change, 6 drachmas 3 obols for charge of receipt, 3 obols. Remainder 561 drachmas, which have been paid into the bank of Apollonios.

title: Loan
summary: Acknowledgement of a loan, for which the lender having in place of interest the right of living for a year in a pastophorion belonging to the borrower.

title: Receipt for various taxes.
summary: Receipt for various taxes paid to the tax collectors of Tebtunis by Thonis.

title: Receipt for naubion ?.
summary: Memorandum of sums paid probably for naubion, impost connected with the construction of dikes and canals, apparently a money payment in lieu of digging.

title: Tax receipt.
summary: Receipt for various taxes.

title: Account (?)
summary: Unknown.

title: Tax receipt.
summary: Receipt for two payments of eight drachmae.
translation_text: 23rd year, for the account of Pachon. Pasion son of Sarapion has paid for the prosodoi of the village Tebtunis (?) on account eight drachmas. (...)

title: Official letter
summary: Remains too meagre to allow reconstruction of content

title: Petition to Flavius N.N. (Strategos)
summary: Unknown.

title: Petition to Bolanos the strategos
summary: The text breaks off after the address.
translation_text: To Bolanos, strategos of the Themistos and Polemon divisions of the Arsinoite nome, from Tephorsais daughter of Turannos the son of Protas from the village of ... (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Account of payments for various purposes.
summary: Account listing payments for various purposes and articles such as wine and bread.

title: Receipt for rent.
summary: Receipt issued by Mystharion to two persons for payment of hay rent.
translation_text: Mustharion son of Herakleides to N.N. son of Herakles and N.N. (and N.N. ?), greeting. I have received from you (...)

title: Account of a vintner
summary: Account of the grapes gathered from different vineyards and of the wine made from them.

title: Account of persons paying temple tax.
summary: Account listing people who pay their temple tax (logeia).

title: Account.
summary: Account listing payments for various purposes, in silver and bronze money.

title: Letter or petition to a strategos.
summary: Fragment with beginning address only.
translation_text: To Tiberius Claudius Chrysermos, strategos. (Here the papyrus breaks off.)

title: Report of a trial
summary: Fragment of the conclusion of a report of a trial before Mamertinus (probably the prefect in 133-135 A.D.). A quittance of the 17th year of Hadrian (132-133 A.D.) is mentioned.

title: Account of bronze drachmas.
summary: List of names and amounts, mentioning payments of 50 and 80 copper drachmae for castor berries and 100 drachmae for wreaths (of victory?)

title: Official letter from Aristippos to Eumelos
summary: Aristippos orders Eumelos to comply with the directions contained in the included letter from Pankrates (overseer of the attribution of land to the catoecic cavalry), regarding the promotion of a holder of cleruchic land.
translation_text: Aristippos to Eumelos, greeting. Appended you will find a copy of the letter written to me by Pankrates, the supervisor of the arrangement of the catoecic cavalry. Endeavor therefore not to trouble Asklepiades with the duties of ephodos. Good-bye. The 36th year, Payni .. Pankrates to Aristippos, greeting. We have appended for your information a copy of the letter written by us to ..., the Basilikos Grammateus. The 36th year, Phamenoth .. To N.N. Appended is a copy of the letter written to us by Sosos and Aigyptios, ... appointed by the polity of the Cretans, relating to the affairs of Asklepiades, in order that you may know of his removal to the fifth hipparchy and that the instructions may be carried out. Sosos and Aigyptos to Pankrates, greeting. Since orders have been given through us that the catoecic cavalry should be ..., Apollodoros, one of the first friends and Epistates and scribe of the catoecic cavalry, has sent me from the 500 men who have been granted the polity of the Cretans, Asklepiades son of Ptolemaios, a Macedonian of the divisional epheboi, on condition that he has a holding of 24 arouras in the neighborhood of Kerkeosiris in the division of Polemon. Kindly therefore register the fact and receive him into the fifth hipparchy of the 100-aroura-men, and communicate with Apollodoros. We have, further, appended the description of Asklepiades and the name of his son. He is about 22 years of age, short, fair, curly-haired ..., his son is Ptolemaios, aged 5 months.

title: Account of oil
summary: Part of an account referring to the manufacture and distribution of oil.
translation_text: Remainder carried to Mesore 143 1/2 1/12 1/24 artabas. In Mesore we received nothing. In Thoth from Touphis 500 art., from which deduct 7 per cent for loss in cleansing, making 32 2/3 art., leaving 467 1/3 art. Of clean sesame by the 30-choinix measure, which makes with what was carried forward 6101/2 1/3 1/12 1/24 art. Of this there was manufactured in Mesore 88 art., in Thoth 188 art., total 276 art.; remainder carried to Phaophi 334 1/2 1/3 1/12 1/24 art., also 22 1/6 art. Of untested croton. In this a loss occurred through Damasippos of 5 1/6 1/24 art., remainder 329 1/2 1/4 art. of clean sesame. Fluid produce. Carried forward from the 29th year to Mecheir of the 30th year 83 metretai 10 choes of sesame oil. Sesame manufactured: Mecheir, 112 art. From which the yield was 38 metr. 6 ch.; Phamenoth, 120 art., yield of oil 41 metr. 3 ch. 6 kotylai; Pharmouthi, 56 art., oil 19 metr. 3 ch.; Pachon, no manufacture; Payni, manufactured 80 art., yield of oil 27 metr. 6 ch.; Epeiph, ... 3 ch. ... Remainder carried to Mesore [- - art.] etc.

title: Account of drachmas
summary: List of names and amounts in drachmas, probably payments.

title: Demotic contract with docket in Greek.
Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for the tax on pigeon houses.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for the tax on the transfer of house property.

title: List of payments to a bank.
summary: List of persons whose names begin with O, who make payments in different months to a bank.

title: Account of receipts and expenditures
summary: Account listing various receipts and expenses (in drachmas) connected with priests.

title: Account of a club.
summary: Account in which payments made by members of a club are listed.

title: Account of a trial.
summary: A trial before an official, apparently named Theon, in which the defendants, Horion and Babinios, seem to have been accused of extortion by certain villagers.

title: Account of crops.
summary: List of names of cultivators of public lands, with crops grown upon their land.

title: Tax list
summary: List of catoeci who pay 1 artaba of wheat or barley upon each aroura of their holdings.

title: List of landholders at Kerkeosiris
summary: Four columns of a list of landholders from Kerkeosiris, the first three listing crown tenants, divided in three groups, the fourth column some cleruchs.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for tax on transfer of property paid to the account of a nomarch.
translation_text: The eighteenth year of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Pius, Phamenoth 29th. Tephorsais daughter of [-]ouphis, Heraklas son of [-]sis, Orsenouphis son of [-]ais and Tephorsais daughter of Orseus, all four from Tebtunis, have paid into the bank at Ptolemais Euergetis in the account of (Tiberius?) Proclus, nomarch, through Demetrios and Sarapion, collectors of the sales-tax, for the 4th share of a house and courtyard in the village Tebtunis, bought from Hermes and Taorsenouphis, both children of Heron, at a price of 2 talents 400 drachmas, one hundred sixty drachmas, total 160 dr. (2nd hand) I, Sarapion, have signed (for the receipt of) the 160 silver drachmas.

title: Receipt for the payment of wheat.
summary: Receipt issued for the payment of 3 3/4 artabae of wheat by Ploution.

title: Contract of receipt
summary: Fragments of an acknowledgement by Eudaemonis and Geminus of the receipt of a sum of money from Pakebkis, being apparently part of an inheritance from their mother.

title: Account of expenditures
summary: Account listing payments made in connection with land.

title: List of names
summary: List of names for an unknown purpose.

title: Account of tax receipts.
summary: A list of persons, arranged according to the days of the month, who pay in the great majority of cases 3 1/2 obols and two obols for ep( ).

title: Account of tax receipts.
summary: A list of persons who pay 4 drachmae.

title: Declaration of pastophoroi.
summary: Statement of returns, followed by a list of persons with their ages.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for tax paid by Pouris.
translation_text: The fourteenth year of Imperator Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius. Tyrannos, ex-epiteretes for the thirteenth year of the priestly sales of Tebtunis, was paid (by) Pouris (?) son of Lous (?) from the sum he owed for the thirteenth year, twelve drachmas, total 12 dr.

title: Private or official letter
summary: Conclusion of a letter of unclear contents.
provenance: crocodile cartonnage at Tebtunis, number unknown
genre: Private letters, Roman period; Business letters, Roman Period
translation_text: ... to remain, I have written to you so that you may come immediately having the money, for because I was waiting for you, I have not yet registered. I am convinced that you have not joined the army. Goodbye. Year 35 of Caesar, Payni 29.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for two payments of the ten-percent tax on calves by the priests of Tebtunis.
translation_text: The 18th year of Lucius Septimius Severus Pius Pertinax and Marcus Aurelius Antoninus Pius, Augusti, and (deleted) Publius Septimius Geta Caesar Augustus (end of deletion), Hadrianus 30. Paid to Apion, nomarch of the Arsinoite nome, acting through his assistant Heron, for the ten-percent tax on calves, for the village of Tebtunis, by the priests, for the receipts of year 18, on account, twenty drachmas, total 20 dr. And on Pharmouthi .. twenty drachmas, total 20 dr.

title: Tax receipt
summary: Receipt for payment of ten-percent tax on calves by the priests of Tebtunis.
translation_text: The ..th year of Lucius Septimius Severus Pertinax Augustus, Thoth 14. Paid to Apion, nomarch of the Arsinoite nome, acting through his representative Horos, for the ten-percent tax on calves, for the village of Tebtunis, by the priests, for the receipts of year 2, on account, twenty drachmas, total 20 dr.

title: Land survey.
summary: Land survey list.

title: Acount of receipts and expenditure.
summary: Account listing receipts and expenditure per day.

title: Receipt for demosia
summary: Ploution acknowledges the receipt of five artabas of wheat owed for public dues.
translation_text: Ploution, collector-designate of grain taxes at Tali to Didymos son of Leontarchos through his children. I have received from you five artabas which you owed for public dues for the crop of the 7th year, which I will measure out ...

title: Tax receipt
summary: Taxes paid by Heracles, in the 13th and 14th years of the Emperor Hadrian for the tax on trades.
translation_text: The thirteenth year of Hadrianus the lord for the account of Epeiph. Herakles son of Paopis has paid to Sokrates and Kastor, collectors of money taxes at Tebtunis, for the tax on trades at Tebtunis of the thirteenth year six drachmas, extra two obols, half obol, charge for receipt . And in the fourteenth year for the account of Pauni, Herakles son of Paopis has paid to Sokrates and Kastor, collectors of money taxes at Tebtunis, for the tax on trades at Tebtunis of the thirteenth year six drachmas, extra two obols, half obol, charge for receipt .

title: Contract
summary: Contract between four persons for a division of land.

title: Purchase of a priestly office.
summary: Purchase of the propheteia of Sokneptunis (?)
date_text: Mid or end of 2nd century A.D.

title: Document concerning priests.
summary: Copy of a document from the public archives concerning the priests at Tebtunis.

title: Petition : draft
summary: Petition from a man connected with farming of taxes who had been arrested and had already spent three years in jail, asking for his release.
translation_text: ... to release them and the guarantors from the deficit. Wherefore at that time they were released from jail (canceled: but the), and the guarantors. After this, Alexander the (canceled: ta(x-)) official in charge of the tax farmers, (canceled: now deceased), lodged a complaint against me, without petition, with Argeios the epimeletes in the year 25, Mesore, fallaciously ... me immediately to deposit (canceled: to deposit) the security ... Argeios took me away to the Big Jail in Krokodilon polis ... and I have up to now been confined already for three years, lacking the necessities. But in fact we were previously acquitted by Apollonios the dioiketes and after that (canceled: they were acquitted) by Queen Cleopatra and King Ptolemy from the charges, mistakes and intentional crimes down to ... of the year 25; and they decreed that no one ... above-written under any pretext whatsoever. Therefore, so that I may not waste away in jail, neglected, contrary to all decency, I beg you with every plea to order ...

title: Receipt for transport dues.
summary: Receipt for three payments by Didume daughter of Herakleides, for a total of 48 drachmae.
translation_text: The 3rd year, Pauni 27th. Didume daughter of Herakleides has paid to Heron, collector of taxes, through Apollonios the assistant for transport charges of the same year thirty two drachmas, total 32 dr. (...) eight drachmas, total 8 dr. (...) 8 dr.

title: Account.
summary: Too little survives of the account to get informed about the contents.

title: List of persons.
summary: Persons who pay (?) various amounts in grain and money.

title: Taxing list.
summary: Headings (5 in all) is followed, in smaller script, by entries of persons in diverse trades at Tebtunis, making various money payments in different months.

title: List of persons.
summary: List of persons, entered per day of a lost month.

title: Receipt for taxes.
summary: A receipt for various taxes paid to the collectors of money taxes at Tebtunis.

title: Unidentified fragment
summary: Document of unknown content.

title: Petition : draft
summary: Draft of a petition from a person connected to tax farming to be released from prison

title: Register of official correspondence.
summary: Fragment of what appears to be copies of the integral parts of official correspondence.

title: Register of official correspondence.
summary: Register of official letters the content of which is unknown.

title: Letter of reproof.
summary: The writer censures his correspondent for incompetence.
translation_text: ... alleging inability to produce them because of their death. It appears, then, that owing to your indifference I shall have to look for the animals that are required but are dead. Has your way of feeding them been to throw the young calves upon the beans, and did you neglect to send ... to Agathon or ...? I do not see how you will be able to look after your own affairs. So take care that I don't come to quarrel with you instead of being on good terms. Good-bye. The 21st year, Epeiph 26.

title: Account of grain.
summary: Account of grain, apparently written on a detached sheet, including recording of payments to individuals.

title: Account of receipts in kind and money.
summary: Payments recorded come under the headings of rent, use of pastures, guards, dikes, and vineyards sown with grain. Includes reference to the dorea of a certain Sosibios, possibly the well-known statesman of that name under Euergetes I.

title: Account of grain.
summary: Apparently concerns alterations to payments made by individuals.

title: Account of receipts in kind
summary: Account of payments received for rent and taxes from crown tenants in various villages, the total of which was partially set aside as seed for the next season, and used as payments to officials.

title: Record of sales.
summary: Record of sales of property, together with amounts paid by the purchasers.
translation_text: ...and the announced period having expired and no person having come forward or offered opposition, was advertised for sale and was sold in one of the komasteria at Krokodilon polis by order of Zopuros in the presence of Dionusios son of Diogenes, one of the friends (of the king), Nikion son of Prokles, hipparch, Diokles son of Zenodoros, divisional head of police, Imouthes, basilikos grammateus, and other customary personages, the crier being Demetrios, in Mecheir of the 23rd year... half a share of a house, [for which] ... in presence of the customary authorities in ... of the 23rd year Petosiris son of Besis offered to pay forthwith 1 talent 2000 drachmas, after which the aforesaid offer having been placarded it was sold in one of the komasteria at Krokodilon polis by order of Zopuros in presence of the customary authorities in Mecheir of the 23rd year to the aforesaid Petosiris, (has paid) forthwith in bronze at a discount 1 talent 2000 drachmas, as sales-tax 400 drachmas, as the 1/60th 133 drachmas 2 obols, as the 1/1000th 8 drachmas. ... and [-]onopis and Thermouthis, all three daughters of Sembes, (have paid) as the price of ... their brothers half share of a house.

title: Account of receipts and expenditures.
summary: Apparently, records receipts related to taxes. The expenditures are small sums given to government employees in the division of Themistes, payments to persons who conducted business, and payments into the bank.

title: Account of receipts and expenditures : recapitulation
summary: Apparently, records receipts related to taxes. The expenditures are small sums given to government employees in the division of Themistes, payments to persons who conducted business, and payments into the bank.

title: Account
summary: Numbers; kites and fractions of kites

title: Account of land.
summary: Detailed account of the landed property of Sosandra, daughter of Heracleides, and value of its different parts.

title: Account of taxes.
summary: Concerns the taxes on millers and sellers of bread and meat in villages of the division of Themistes.

title: Privy purse account.
summary: Early example of privy purse account.

title: Official account of receipts and expenditures.
summary: Receipts consist largely of the proceeds of sales of wine and of arrear. Expenditues include freight and carriage of wine, loans, salaries, payments to government officials, and various others.

title: Account of receipts in money.
summary: Lists monies received for dues on vineyards. Apparently certain tools of those in default of their payments have been seized as security.

title: Account of receipts in kind
summary: List of payment of wheat and olyra by crown tenants for rents, repayments of loans and seed, and arrears from the preceding seasons.

title: List of tax-payers.
summary: Gives names of various tax-payers.
translation_text: Psenechotis, son of Phanemieus Tneferos, wife Phanemieus, brother To..., son of... Soeris, wife Phathemis, brother Horos the younger, son of Horos Esersuthis, wife Harpaesis, son of Petobastis Tarmouthis, wife Petoys, son of Marres Taubastis, wife Aryotes, son of Teos Artemidoros, brother Phratres, brother ... Petabthis, brother ... Col. iii Paglos Thaibis, wife Haryotes, son of Pete.erth[ Thasis, wife Haryotes, son of Pan... Harpaesis, son of Phar... Esenremphis, wife Petosiris, son of Petep... Peteesis, son of Komoapis Tanthyphis, wife Thotortaios... Har...ys, son of Teos ...wife Kalatutis... Tagos, wife Serepis... Harsythes

title: Account.
summary: Portion of a private account, including 158 drachmae for travel provisions.

title: Designation of an Alexandrian citizen.
summary: One person, Leon, Son of Leon, and possibly others, listed as citizens of Alexandria.

title: list of sheep and goats
summary: A fragmentary list of sheep and goats belonging to the inhabitants of Samareia in the Arsinoite nome, probably founded as a Jewish settlement in the 3rd century B.C.

title: List of swine.
summary: List of swine owned by the inhabitants of Phanesios Akra (presumably identical with Phaneseos Kome)

title: Account of expenditures.
summary: Details money received by the writer and its expenditure in food and wages.

title: Account of household expenses.
summary: Part of an account of daily expenses. In copper drachmae, at the ratio of copper to silver of about 1:60.

title: Account of agricultural expenses.
summary: Lists workmen's wages, hire of oxen, and other expenses during the ploughing season at certain villages of the division of Themistes. It appears that after the beginning of the 2nd century B.C. laborers' wages increased. The verso includes also a grain account, running parallel to the money account.

title: Account of travelling expenses.
summary: An account of the daily expenses of three men who were travelling by boat down the river. Prices given are from the period of the copper standard.

title: Correspondence concerning a sitologos
summary: Official letter from Alexandros to Philon, enclosing copies of a letter sent by Alexandros to Aniketos and of a memorandum received from him.
translation_text: Alexandros to Philon, greeting. Below is a copy of a letter to my agent Aniketos. You will accordingly do well to issue orders yourself that the persons are not to be molested before I arrive and give their statements proper attention. Goodbye. The 19th year, Pha... To Aniketos. Below is a copy of the memorandum presented to me by Ammonios, Sitologos of Boubastos in the division of Herakleides. Let safe-conducts be given to the persons specified and let them remain at the duty assigned to them until I arrive on the spot and give due attention to their assertions. To Alexandros, epimeletes, from Ammonios, sitologos of Boubastos in the division of Herakleides. ... presented a memorandum to Bakchon the dioiketes in which he declared that he owed [the account?] of the ... year a further amount of 2500 artabas of wheat and [would pay?] through a stated agent (?) 5000 art. of wheat, free from unsubstantial receipts or other impostures, believing that he would be cleared of the debt... (Addressed on the verso) To the archiphylakites Philon. Aresono( ?)

title: Account of expenditures
summary: An account, probably from the reign of Philometor, of the money spent by certain dependents or agents of a well-to-do household.

title: Acccount of debt?
summary: List of names, against each of which is placed a sum, followed in some cases by a deduction representing apparently partial payment.

title: Account of expenditure.
summary: A variety of household expenses.

title: Apollonios' letter : to the village officials.
Contractor's letter : fragment : to the officials of a village.
summary: Apparently concerns unproductive land.

title: Masyllus' letter : to the police of Oxyrhyncha.
summary: Concerns malefactors.

title: Letter : draft.
summary: Letter concerning a meeting between the epimeletes and the donkey-drivers of Boubastos.

title: Pasis' letter : to the police.
summary: Pasis, comarch of Oxyrhyncha, with the elders of the cultivators, writes to Tryphwn and the police.

title: Letter.
summary: The writer speals about the cleansing of a canal, and about the weight of tools supplied for the work.

title: Demotic document
summary: Amounts of wheat and measurements of arouras.

title: Account of revenue from fish
summary: Account of revenue from fish collected on behalf of the government by various persons in various locales.

title: Letter.
summary: End of a letter apparently concerning laborers and wages.

title: Letter : fragment
summary: Part of a letter with a reference to the price of wheat.

title: Register of house-property
summary: Register of owners of houses, each name being followed by a brief description of the property, its sizes, and its value in talents and bronze drachmas.

title: Petesis' letter : fragments : to Ptolemaios.
summary: Petesis orders the delivery of certain quantities of wheat on payment of the price.

title: Payment order.
summary: A payment order emanating from a dioeketes and probably transmitted to a banker.

title: Document concerning a dispute.
summary: Concerns a dispute about a lease of land under reclamation.

title: Household account.
summary: A daily account of household expenses, kept in copper drachmae. Possibly the estate referred to is at Philadelphia.

title: Report concerning conflict with robbers : fragment.
Letter : fragment.
summary: Concerns the collection of dues.

title: Official communication : fragment
summary: Concerns some occurrence, possibly an uprising, in the inerior of Egypt.

title: Register of land
summary: Short report detailing the situation with regard to the crown land of Kerkeosiris on Nov. 9, 114; text written only a few days latter.
translation_text: Year 4. Report from Menches, komogrammateus of Kerkeosiris, on the watering and sowing of the land through Paophi 20. In year 3, 1193 3/4 arourae of land, the rent of which is 4665 5/12, and out of these 1122 1/4 arourai at 4313 11/12 artabae have been watered through Paophi 20. The remaining arourae on which water is standing are 71 1/2 at 351 1/2 artabae. And out of the watered portion were sown with lentil 9 arourae at 44 1/4 artabae, with arakos 35 arourae at 167 1/2 artabae; pasturage: 60 arourae at 60 artabae. They total 104 arourae at 271 3/4 artabae. The remainder which are being seeded in due order (are) 1018 1/4 arourae at 4042 1/6.

title: Register of land
summary: List of surface areas of plots of land, under separate headings.
